text: Four robotic 8mm ports and one assistant 12mm port are positioned. During hilar dissection, the right and left hepatic ducts in the stenotic area are clearly visible. Old hemostatic clips are removed from the ductus, and polymer clips near the common hepatic duct and hilum are extracted.
The surgeon transects the left hepatic duct, followed by resection of the common duct below the stenosis using a GIAS tapering device.
The surgeon sutures the right and left hepatic ducts together, creating a double parallel configuration where the ducts are connected side-by-side.

The surgeon constructs the small intestine 30 centimeters distal from the Treitz ligament, pulling the distal limb upwards to prepare for the hepatico-jejunostomy. Using the double barrel technique, an end-to-side hepatico-jejunostomy anastomosis is performed, with the posterior layers sutured together using a running stitch with absorbable V-lock sutures.

The surgeon sutures the anterior layers of the anastomosis using a V-lock technique, applying gradual tension with two separate sutures. A resorbable two-zero barbed wire is used for the outer layer, while a three-zero barbed wire is used for the inner layer.
